examples.title: NPV Calculation Algorithm
examples.description: Calculates Net Present Value for a software project
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_NPV
INPUT: initialInvestment, cashFlows[], discountRate, projectLife
OUTPUT: npv

BEGIN
 npv = -initialInvestment
 FOR year = 1 TO projectLife DO
 pvFactor = 1 / (1 + discountRate)^year
 pv = cashFlows[year] * pvFactor
 npv = npv + pv
 END FOR
 RETURN npv
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates NPV by first initializing with negative initial investment, then for each year calculating the present value factor and multiplying by the cash flow to get present value, and summing all present values.

examples.title: Payback Period Calculation
examples.description: Determines the time required to recover initial investment
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_Payback_Period
INPUT: initialInvestment, cashFlows[], projectLife
OUTPUT: paybackPeriod

BEGIN
 cumulative = 0
 FOR year = 1 TO projectLife DO
 cumulative = cumulative + cashFlows[year]
 IF cumulative >= initialInvestment THEN
 fractionalYear = (initialInvestment - (cumulative - cashFlows[year])) / cashFlows[year]
 paybackPeriod = (year - 1) + fractionalYear
 RETURN paybackPeriod
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN -1 // Investment not recovered
END
examples.explanation: Calculates cumulative cash flows year by year and determines when the initial investment is recovered. Uses interpolation for fractional years.

examples.title: Benefit-Cost Ratio Calculation
examples.description: Calculates BCR to determine project viability
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_BCR
INPUT: costs[], benefits[], discountRate, projectLife
OUTPUT: bcr

BEGIN
 presentValueCosts = 0
 presentValueBenefits = 0
 
 // Calculate present value of costs
 FOR year = 0 TO projectLife DO
 pvFactor = 1 / (1 + discountRate)^year
 presentValueCosts = presentValueCosts + (costs[year] * pvFactor)
 presentValueBenefits = presentValueBenefits + (benefits[year] * pvFactor)
 END FOR
 
 bcr = presentValueBenefits / presentValueCosts
 RETURN bcr
END
examples.explanation: Computes present value of all costs and benefits over project life, then divides benefits by costs to get the ratio.

examples.title: IRR Estimation using Iteration
examples.description: Estimates Internal Rate of Return through iterative approach
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_IRR
INPUT: initialInvestment, cashFlows[], projectLife
OUTPUT: irr

BEGIN
 lowRate = 0.0
 highRate = 1.0
 tolerance = 0.0001
 maxIterations = 100
 
 FOR i = 1 TO maxIterations DO
 midRate = (lowRate + highRate) / 2
 npv = Calculate_NPV(initialInvestment, cashFlows, midRate, projectLife)
 
 IF npv > 0 THEN
 lowRate = midRate
 ELSE
 highRate = midRate
 END IF
 
 IF ABS(npv) < tolerance THEN
 RETURN midRate * 100 // Convert to percentage
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN midRate * 100
END
examples.explanation: Uses binary search to find the discount rate where NPV equals zero. Iterates until NPV is within tolerance threshold.
